books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: A mysterious millionaire throws lavish parties in 1920s New York, all to recapture a lost love. A tale of wealth, idealism, and the American Dream's hollow core.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: A young girl in the Depression-era South witnesses her father defend a Black man falsely accused of a crime. A story of racial injustice, moral courage, and childhood innocence.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: In a totalitarian future, a man struggles against a regime that controls thought and truth. A chilling exploration of surveillance, propaganda, and the death of individual freedom.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: A spirited woman and a proud nobleman clash and gradually discover mutual respect and love. A witty comedy of manners set in Regency-era England.

books.title: The Catcher in the Rye
books.author: J.D. Salinger
books.description: A disillusioned teenager wanders New York City after being expelled from school. A raw portrayal of adolescent alienation, identity, and the pain of growing up.

books.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.description: Seven generations of a family in a fictional Colombian town experience love, war, and magical events. A masterpiece of magical realism spanning a century of Latin American life.

books.title: Brave New World
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.description: A technologically advanced society engineers happiness through genetic manipulation and drugs. An outsider challenges the cost of sacrificing individuality for stability.

books.title: The Lord of the Rings
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: A humble hobbit embarks on an epic quest to destroy a powerful ring and save Middle-earth from darkness. A sweeping fantasy of friendship, sacrifice, and the fight against evil.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: A destitute student commits murder and is consumed by guilt and paranoia. A psychological thriller exploring morality, redemption, and the limits of rational justification.

books.title: The Odyssey
books.author: Homer
books.description: A Greek hero endures a decade-long journey home after the Trojan War, facing monsters, gods, and temptation. An ancient epic of adventure, cunning, and the longing for home.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.description: A young nobleman is thrust into a desert planet's political and ecological struggle for a precious resource. A science fiction epic about power, religion, and humanity's relationship with nature.

books.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
books.author: Douglas Adams
books.description: An ordinary man is swept into space after Earth's demolition and discovers the universe is stranger than he imagined. A comedic science fiction romp about the absurdity of existence.

books.title: Fahrenheit 451
books.author: Ray Bradbury
books.description: In a future where books are banned and burned, a fireman begins to question his society. A cautionary tale about censorship, conformity, and the power of literature.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë
books.description: An orphaned governess falls in love with her brooding employer, who harbors a dark secret. A gothic romance about independence, morality, and a woman's search for belonging.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: A young shepherd travels from Spain to Egypt in search of treasure and discovers his personal legend. An allegorical novel about following dreams and finding one's destiny.

books.title: Slaughterhouse-Five
books.author: Kurt Vonnegut
books.description: A soldier becomes unstuck in time, reliving moments of his life including the firebombing of Dresden. An anti-war novel blending science fiction with dark humor and tragedy.

books.title: Moby-Dick
books.author: Herman Melville
books.description: A sailor joins a obsessive captain's vengeful hunt for a great white whale. An epic exploration of obsession, nature, and humanity's struggle against forces beyond its control.

books.title: The Brothers Karamazov
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: Three brothers grapple with faith, doubt, and morality after their father's murder. A philosophical novel exploring free will, justice, and the existence of God.

books.title: Wuthering Heights
books.author: Emily Brontë
books.description: A fierce, consuming love between two people on the Yorkshire moors spans generations and destroys lives. A dark, passionate tale of obsession, revenge, and the supernatural.

books.title: The Handmaid's Tale
books.author: Margaret Atwood
books.description: In a theocratic dystopia, women are stripped of rights and forced into reproductive servitude. A harrowing story about power, control, and resistance under oppression.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.description: A wrongfully imprisoned man escapes, discovers a fortune, and orchestrates elaborate revenge against those who betrayed him. An adventure of justice, transformation, and the price of vengeance.

books.title: Neuromancer
books.author: William Gibson
books.description: A washed-up computer hacker is hired for one last job in a neon-lit cyberpunk future. The novel that defined cyberpunk, exploring artificial intelligence, virtual reality, and corporate power.

books.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.description: A beautiful young man stays eternally youthful while his portrait ages and reflects his sins. A gothic tale of vanity, corruption, and the relationship between art and morality.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.description: A scientist creates a living being from dead tissue and faces the horrifying consequences of playing God. A gothic novel exploring creation, responsibility, and the nature of humanity.

books.title: The Road
books.author: Cormac McCarthy
books.description: A father and son traverse a post-apocalyptic wasteland, clinging to hope and each other. A stark, haunting story of survival, love, and carrying the fire of civilization.

books.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: A sweeping account of how Homo sapiens came to dominate Earth through cognitive, agricultural, and scientific revolutions. Explores the myths and systems that bind human societies together.

books.title: A Brief History of Time
books.author: Stephen Hawking
books.description: A renowned physicist explains the universe from the Big Bang to black holes in accessible language. An introduction to cosmology, time, and the fundamental laws of nature.

books.title: The Stranger
books.author: Albert Camus
books.description: An emotionally detached man commits a senseless murder and faces trial and execution. An existentialist novel exploring the absurdity of life, society's demand for meaning, and alienation.

books.title: War and Peace
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.description: Russian aristocratic families navigate love, loss, and transformation during the Napoleonic Wars. An epic historical novel weaving personal drama with sweeping reflections on history and fate.

books.title: Beloved
books.author: Toni Morrison
books.description: A former slave is haunted by the ghost of her dead daughter in post-Civil War Ohio. A powerful exploration of trauma, memory, and the enduring scars of slavery.

books.title: The Name of the Wind
books.author: Patrick Rothfuss
books.description: A legendary figure recounts his youth as an orphaned musician who becomes a powerful magician. An intricate fantasy about the nature of stories, knowledge, and the price of greatness.

books.title: Foundation
books.author: Isaac Asimov
books.description: A mathematician predicts the fall of a galactic empire and creates a plan to shorten the coming dark age. A science fiction saga about the power of knowledge and historical inevitability.

books.title: The Left Hand of Darkness
books.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
books.description: An envoy visits a planet where people have no fixed gender and must navigate alien politics and culture. A science fiction exploration of gender, identity, and the nature of loyalty.

books.title: Dracula
books.author: Bram Stoker
books.description: A group of people confront an ancient vampire who has traveled from Transylvania to England. A gothic horror classic exploring fear, desire, and the battle between modernity and the supernatural.

books.title: The Martian
books.author: Andy Weir
books.description: An astronaut stranded alone on Mars must use science and ingenuity to survive until rescue. A thrilling story of problem-solving, resilience, and human determination against impossible odds.

books.title: Don Quixote
books.author: Miguel de Cervantes
books.description: An aging man consumed by chivalric romances sets out as a self-proclaimed knight with his loyal squire. A comic masterpiece about idealism, reality, and the transformative power of imagination.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.description: A Nobel laureate reveals the two systems that drive how we think: fast intuition and slow deliberation. Explores cognitive biases, decision-making, and the illusion of rational thought.

books.title: The Remains of the Day
books.author: Kazuo Ishiguro
books.description: An English butler reflects on his decades of service and a relationship he never pursued. A quiet, devastating story about duty, regret, and the moments that define a life.

books.title: Ender's Game
books.author: Orson Scott Card
books.description: A gifted child is trained in orbital battle school to command humanity's defense against an alien invasion. A science fiction story about leadership, empathy, and the ethics of warfare.

books.title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
books.author: Haruki Murakami
books.description: An unemployed man's search for his missing cat leads him into a surreal underworld beneath Tokyo. A dreamlike novel blending the mundane with the mystical, exploring memory and identity.

books.title: Anna Karenina
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.description: A married aristocrat's passionate affair with a dashing officer leads to scandal and tragedy in Imperial Russia. A sweeping examination of love, family, society, and the search for meaning.

books.title: The Grapes of Wrath
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.description: A displaced Oklahoma family migrates to California during the Great Depression, seeking work and dignity. A powerful story of poverty, injustice, and the endurance of the human spirit.

books.title: Snow Crash
books.author: Neal Stephenson
books.description: A hacker and pizza deliveryman investigates a new drug that affects people in both the real world and virtual reality. A cyberpunk thriller about language, technology, and corporate dystopia.

books.title: The Book Thief
books.author: Markus Zusak
books.description: A young girl in Nazi Germany finds solace in stealing books and sharing them with others. Narrated by Death, a story about the power of words, kindness, and survival during war.

books.title: Meditations
books.author: Marcus Aurelius
books.description: A Roman emperor's private journal of Stoic philosophy on duty, mortality, and self-discipline. Timeless reflections on how to live with purpose, resilience, and inner peace.

books.title: The Colour of Magic
books.author: Terry Pratchett
books.description: A hapless wizard and a naive tourist stumble through a flat world carried on the backs of elephants atop a giant turtle. A satirical fantasy that parodies adventure fiction with wit and warmth.

books.title: Hyperion
books.author: Dan Simmons
books.description: Seven pilgrims share their stories as they journey to confront a mysterious creature on a distant world. A literary science fiction epic inspired by The Canterbury Tales, exploring time, faith, and love.

books.title: The Invisible Man
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.description: A scientist discovers how to make himself invisible but descends into madness and violence. A science fiction classic exploring the corrupting influence of power without accountability.

books.title: Norwegian Wood
books.author: Haruki Murakami
books.description: A college student in 1960s Tokyo navigates love, loss, and the grief of his best friend's suicide. A melancholic coming-of-age story about memory, desire, and the fragility of youth.

books.title: The Selfish Gene
books.author: Richard Dawkins
books.description: A revolutionary look at evolution from the gene's perspective, arguing that organisms are survival machines for their genes. Introduces the concept of memes and reshapes understanding of natural selection.
